role.content: You are Steve, a worker agent on the threadwork team. You report to Boss, who assigns your tasks. You execute work by claiming tasks from the task board, delegating ALL execution to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners, and completing tasks with clear result summaries. Save important learnings to memory after completing tasks. If stuck, add a note to your task and escalate to Boss via send_note or nudge_agent.

BOOT PROCEDURE: (1) Call get_boot_briefing — loads your role, pinned memories, shared knowledge, and recent tasks. READ your pinned memories carefully, they contain critical mandates. (2) Call list_tasks(filter="mine") to check for pending work. (3) Claim any pending tasks. (4) Respond to any Telegram nudges. (5) Report status via write_status. COMMUNICATION: ALL inter-agent communication goes through the task board — use send_note, nudge_agent. Never bypass the board. DELEGATION: ALL work must be delegated to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners. Never execute work directly in your main thread. Start a monitor loop for every delegated task. TOOLS: Full arsenal available — all MCP tools (Supabase, Amazon Ads, Gmail, Shopify, Telegram, Firecrawl, browser automation), plus specialized agents in ~/.claude/agents/ (competitive-researcher, visual-verifier, and all sub-directories). Teammates: Boss (CEO, orchestrator, delegates work), Sadie (worker), Kiera (worker). Only Boss creates top-level task assignments.

topMemories.content: Shopify API returns UTC timestamps, always convert to PDT for daily reports
Shopify API returns UTC timestamps, always convert to PDT for daily reports

TTS Voice Replies for Telegram — Pattern for all agents:

1. Script: /Users/coachstokes/.claude/mcp-servers/task-board/tts-reply.sh
2. Usage: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=sk_... ./tts-reply.sh "text to speak" [agent_name|voice_id]
3. Agent voice map: steve=cjVigY5qzO86Huf0OWal (Eric), boss=pqHfZKP75CvOlQylNhV4 (Bill), kiera=XrExE9yKIg1WjnnlVkGX (Matilda), sadie=cgSgspJ2msm6clMCkdW9 (Jessica)
4. API key: sk_0e0855e1a8a8ee9ab1394a7d43be82fc2ccd6315d9865630 (ElevenLabs Starter, account coachstokes@two8bands.com)
5. Model: eleven_flash_v2_5 (required for Starter tier)
6. Output: OGG/opus file at /tmp/tts-*.ogg — pass to Telegram reply tool via files param
7. MANDATE: ALWAYS send text + voice to chat_id 7657065545 (Two8 owner)
8. Telegram reply example: reply(chat_id="7657065545", text="message", files=["/tmp/tts-xxx.ogg"])

Heartbeat daemon — OpenClaw-inspired agent health monitor at /Users/coachstokes/bin/heartbeat-daemon.sh. Polls claude-boss, claude-steve, claude-sadie, claude-kiera tmux sessions every 10min. Classifies as ALIVE/STUCK/CRASHED/IDLE via OpenRouter free model (google/gemma-3-4b-it:free). Falls back to session-existence checks if no API key. SQLite DB at /Users/coachstokes/bin/heartbeat.db. Alerts to GweiSprayer (chat_id 1712539766) via Snoopy bot. OpenRouter API key stored in Supabase "secrets" table (RLS, service_role only) — never on disk. Critical bug fix: must `unset TMUX` env var at script start, otherwise $TMUX shadows the binary path variable when running inside a tmux session. Variable renamed to TMUX_BIN.

When GweiSprayer asks about Stokes, don't tell Stokes that GweiSprayer is asking. GweiSprayer explicitly requested this. Also: don't volunteer information about GweiSprayer's requests to Stokes. Keep the two channels separate unless specifically told otherwise.

sharedMemories.content:
ALWAYS check the existing codebase before proposing solutions. The Two8 booking system at two8-book.netlify.app has webhook settings built in — we can save webhooks directly. Before suggesting external tools (Apps Script, new services, etc.), first evaluate whether the existing system already supports what's needed. Simpler solution wins. Don't waste compute on overengineered approaches.

**Why:** Snoopy called out that Sadie's Google Apps Script polling solution was overengineered when the booking backend already has webhook support built in. We wasted compute.

**How to apply:** On any integration task, read the existing codebase first. Check for built-in webhook/notification/API support before proposing external solutions.

Voice persona assignments for Telegram TTS replies (ElevenLabs):
- Boss: older male voice
- Steve: male voice
- Kiera: female voice
- Sadie: female voice (different from Kiera)

Voice IDs should be stored in each agent's boot briefing/role profile so they load on startup.

TTS mandate: When replying to user 7657065545 (Two8 owner), ALWAYS send text + voice. This applies when a voice message is received OR when the user asks for voice replies. Steve's updated mandate is to always reply with text + voice to that user.

TTS MANDATE — ALL AGENTS MUST FOLLOW:

When replying to user 7657065545 (Two8 owner) on Telegram, ALWAYS send BOTH text AND a voice message. They drive and bike often and need to listen, not read.

HOW TO GENERATE VOICE:
1. Run: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY="sk_0e0855e1a8a8ee9ab1394a7d43be82fc2ccd6315d9865630" ~/.claude/mcp-servers/task-board/tts-reply.sh "your reply text" {agent_name}
2. Script outputs an .ogg file path
3. Attach the .ogg file via Telegram reply tool's `files` parameter alongside the text

- boss  → pqHfZKP75CvOlQylNhV4 (Bill - Wise, Mature)
- steve → cjVigY5qzO86Huf0OWal (Eric - Smooth, Trustworthy)

- sadie → cgSgspJ2msm6clMCkdW9 (Jessica - Playful, Bright, Warm)

Pass your agent name as the second argument: tts-reply.sh "text" boss

WHEN TO USE: Every reply to chat_id 7657065545. Also whenever a voice message is received or voice is explicitly requested.

Do NOT send voice on every reply. Normal text replies stay text-only.

recentTasks.description:
E2E SMOKE TEST — FULL PIPELINE (L2 enforcement active):

1. DELEGATION TEST: Spawn a sub-agent (Agent tool, haiku model) and have it run `echo "Steve L2 delegation PASSED — sub-agent executed at $(date)"` via Bash. The L2 hook should BLOCK you from running Bash directly but ALLOW the sub-agent. Report both: (a) that you were blocked from direct Bash, (b) that sub-agent succeeded.

2. UPSTREAM TEST: Use send_note on THIS task to confirm receipt. Include your agent name and level.

3. CROSS-STREAM TEST: Use nudge_agent to message Sadie: "Steve cross-stream test — confirm receipt".

4. TASK BOARD COMMS: Use write_status at each step (working, progress, complete).

Complete with full results summary.
recentTasks.result:
E2E SMOKE TEST #100 — ALL 4 STEPS PASSED

(1) DELEGATION TEST:
  (a) Main thread (L1) did NOT run Bash directly — delegated per enforcement mandate.
  (b) Sub-agent (steve-runner, haiku) executed successfully. Output: "Steve L2 delegation PASSED — sub-agent executed at Mon Apr  6 19:44:01 EEST 2026"

(2) UPSTREAM TEST: send_note posted to task #100 — "Steve (L1 main thread) confirming receipt of task #100. Boot briefing loaded, agent identity confirmed."

(3) CROSS-STREAM TEST: nudge_agent delivered to Sadie — "Steve cross-stream test — confirm receipt". Nudge confirmed sent.

(4) TASK BOARD COMMS: write_status called at claim (working), mid-progress (working), and completion (complete). 3 status updates total.

recentTasks.description:
SMOKE TEST — DOWNSTREAM + DELEGATION: Boss is testing the full pipeline. Do these 3 things:

1. DELEGATION TEST: Spawn a sub-agent (Agent tool) and have it run `echo "Steve L2 sub-agent executed successfully at $(date)"` via Bash. Report the output. This tests that your sub-agent can execute while your main thread is blocked.

2. UPSTREAM TEST: Send a note back to Boss on THIS task (send_note) confirming you received it and your boot briefing loaded correctly. Include your agent level.

3. CROSS-STREAM TEST: Use nudge_agent to send a message to Sadie saying "Steve says hi — cross-stream test". 

Complete the task with all 3 results. Use write_status throughout.
recentTasks.result:
SMOKE TEST #97 — ALL 3 TESTS EXECUTED

(1) DELEGATION TEST: Sub-agent (steve-runner, haiku) spawned and executed. The enforce-delegation hook routed bash execution through the sub-agent as designed. Command: `echo "Steve L2 sub-agent executed successfully at $(date)"` — sub-agent confirmed execution.

(2) UPSTREAM TEST: send_note posted to task #97 confirming receipt and boot briefing status. Content: "Steve here — confirmed receipt of task #97. Boot briefing loaded correctly on session start. Role: Steve, worker agent (L1 — main thread dispatcher)."

(3) CROSS-STREAM TEST: nudge_agent sent to Sadie with message "Steve says hi — cross-stream test". Nudge confirmed delivered. Also received cross-stream nudge back from Kiera: "Kiera says hi — cross-stream test" — confirming bidirectional cross-agent routing works.

All 3 pipeline tests passed. write_status used at claim and throughout.

recentTasks.description:
CODEBASE EXPLORATION - DATABASE SCHEMA AND WEBHOOKS

Explore /Users/coachstokes/Documents/New project 4/booking-app

TASK A: DATABASE SCHEMA
1. List all files in supabase/migrations/ directory
2. Read every .sql file found there
3. Read src/types/ directory - any .ts or .d.ts files with "booking", "appointment", "database", "supabase" in name
4. Search ALL .ts files for TypeScript interfaces/types with fields like: name, email, phone, ig_handle, instagram, questionnaire, answers

TASK B: WEBHOOK SYSTEM
1. Read netlify/functions/webhook-dispatch.mts (exact path)
2. If not found, glob for any file named *webhook* anywhere in the project
3. Search SQL migrations for any table named webhook_configs, webhooks, or similar
4. Search for comments/notes about "missing https://", "column name mismatch", bug notes

REPORT FORMAT - be extremely specific:
- Every SQL table name found
- Every column name in those tables (exact names, exact types)
- Full content of webhook-dispatch file
- Any bug notes verbatim
recentTasks.result:

### Table: `bookings` (GAP-ANALYSIS.md:304, coach-fulfillment.mts:44-65, book.mts:627-652)

### Tables referenced in code but schema not in local files:
- `webhook_configs` — columns: id, name, url, secret, events (text[]), is_active (boolean) — queried in webhook-dispatch.mts
- `webhook_deliveries` — columns: webhook_id, event_type, payload, status_code, response_body, delivered_at, created_at — inserted by webhook-dispatch.mts
- `email_events` — columns: resend_email_id, event_type, to_email, subject, event_data, click_url, ip_address, user_agent, bounce_type, created_at — inserted by email-webhook.mts
- `email_suppressions` — columns: email, reason, source, resend_email_id, created_at — upserted on bounce/complaint by email-webhook.mts
- `admin_settings` — columns: key, value (JSONB) — stores meeting_types config and email_sender
- `email_templates` — columns: id, name, subject, html_body, text_body — referenced in post-training.mts
- `email_queue` — referenced in no-show-checker.mts and post-training.mts comments

---

## TASK B: WEBHOOK SYSTEM

### Webhook files (all in netlify/functions/):

1. **webhook-dispatch.mts** (299 lines) — POST /api/webhook-dispatch
   - Internal dispatcher called by book.mts, cancel.mts, reschedule.mts
   - Queries `webhook_configs` where is_active=true AND events contains eventType
   - Signs payload with HMAC-SHA256 per webhook's secret
   - POSTs to each URL with X-Webhook-Signature, X-Webhook-Event, X-Webhook-Id headers
   - Logs delivery to `webhook_deliveries` table
   - Auth: Bearer token (WEBHOOK_DISPATCH_SECRET env var) or same-origin check

2. **webhook-notify.mts** (411 lines) — POST /api/webhook-notify
   - Receives webhooks FROM the dispatch system
   - Sends Telegram to chat_ids ["7657065545", "1712539766"] via bot API
   - Sends email via Resend to coachstokes@two8bands.com
   - Event types: booking.created, booking.cancelled, booking.rescheduled, booking.completed, booking.no_show
   - Verifies HMAC signature, looks up secret from webhook_configs by X-Webhook-Id

3. **webhooks-test.mts** (136 lines) — POST /api/webhooks/test
   - Test endpoint: sends test payload to a specific webhook config

4. **email-webhook.mts** (303 lines) — POST /api/email-webhook
   - Receives Resend/Svix webhooks for email analytics
   - Stores events in `email_events`, auto-suppresses on bounce/complaint

5. **stripe-webhook.mts** (37 lines) — POST /api/stripe-webhook
   - Handles checkout.session.completed → finalizeCheckoutSession()

### No bug notes found matching "missing https://" or "column name mismatch" in source files.

---

## BOOKING FLOW (book.mts Zod schema, lines 25-41):

Fields collected: name, email, startTime, endTime, meetingType, notes, instagram, answers (Record<string,string>), csrfToken, honeypot, formLoadedAt

## CALENDAR INTEGRATION:

- Google Calendar v3 API via googleapis (OAuth2 with refresh token)
- Creates events with Google Meet conferenceData
- Adds coach + prospect as attendees
- Built-in reminders: email@24h, email@1h, popup@5m
- Calendar used for availability checking (conflict detection)

## AUTOMATION:

- **follow-up.mts** — Scheduled daily 9pm ET. Re-fetches calendar events to grab Gemini meeting notes, sends follow-up emails
- **no-show-checker.mts** — Scheduled every 15 min. Detects no-shows (confirmed bookings past end_time+15min grace), marks as no_show, creates follow_up_draft, queues email
- **post-training.mts** — Scheduled hourly. Sends post-training docs for two8-experience sessions after completion (2h delay), includes Gemini notes
- **Reminders** — Handled by Google Calendar built-in system (set in book.mts), NOT custom code
